summary: Matched Dr. Mario fighter code by deleting the remaining asm file for ft/chara/ftdrmario and replacing it with C across ftdrmario.c plus a small separate translation unit, ftdrmario_2.c. The separate TU was intentional: the PR author stated func_80149954 had to be split out to get correct relocations for a function pointer it uses. The change also expanded ftdrmario.h prototypes and ftDrMarioAttributes, added Fighter struct fields needed by this code, and updated build lists so ftdrmario.o and ftdrmario_2.o are built from src rather than asm. Reviewer feedback highlighted that a placeholder '_2' filename should ideally be replaced by a Special-specific name, probably SpecialN, because ft files should be split by Specials.

key_files.role: deleted 466-line assembly implementation after matching Dr. Mario in C
main matched C implementation; added ftDrMario_OnLoad and many func_80149540 through func_80149910 routines
new separate translation unit for func_80149954 and related small wrappers, created to satisfy relocation behavior for a function pointer
added ftDrMarioAttributes and prototypes for the newly matched Dr. Mario functions
added Fighter fields used by Dr. Mario code, including x10, x21DC, x21E0, x21E4, and adjusted filler layout around ftcmd vars
added ftdrmario.o and ftdrmario_2.o to epilogue unscheduled object list
replaced asm/melee/ft/chara/ftdrmario.o with src/melee/ft/chara/ftdrmario_2.o in TEXT_O_FILES

what_changed: Removed asm/melee/ft/chara/ftdrmario.s entirely and moved the implementation into C.

Expanded src/melee/ft/chara/ftdrmario.c from only ftDrMario_OnDeath to include ftDrMario_OnLoad and multiple helper/action functions such as func_80149540, func_80149560, func_801497CC, func_80149844, func_801498A0, func_801498EC, and func_80149910.
Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftdrmario_2.c containing func_80149954 plus wrapper functions func_80149A6C, func_80149A8C, and func_80149AAC.
Added a ftDrMarioAttributes struct with fields/padding through x14, used by ftDrMario_OnLoad for special attributes.
Added prototypes in ftdrmario.h for the matched functions and explicitly grouped the ftdrmario_2.c functions under a comment.
Extended Fighter layout in fighter.h to expose fields used by this code, including the action/state field at x10 and callback-like fields at x21DC and x21E4.
Updated e_files.mk and obj_files.mk so the new C objects are linked and the old Dr. Mario asm object is no longer used.
smart_moves: The author split func_80149954 into a separate translation unit specifically to get correct relocations for a function pointer, rather than forcing all matched C into a single file.
The C keeps direct evidence from the assembly shape where needed: unused stack arrays, explicit NULL checks, and simple wrapper functions are preserved to maintain matching behavior.
The build-system update was complete enough to remove the old asm object while adding both new C objects, avoiding a mixed duplicate implementation.

Adding the Fighter fields and Dr. Mario attribute struct turned raw offsets such as x21DC, x21E4, x2200_ftcmd_var0, x2200_ftcmd_var1, and x2240 into reusable structure knowledge for later fighter work.
decomp_lessons: If a matched function stores a function pointer and relocation type/order is wrong, splitting the function into its own translation unit can be a valid matching tactic.
Do not assume one source file per old asm file is always sufficient; object boundaries can matter for relocations in this codebase.

When adding placeholder TUs for matching, expect naming review: fighter character files should eventually be split according to the move/Special they implement rather than named generically with suffixes like _2.
Small wrapper functions that only call an existing engine/fighter helper may still need to exist as separate symbols to match the original binary.
Struct/header progress often accompanies fighter matching: exposing just enough fields in Fighter and character attribute structs can make raw-offset C readable while preserving layout.
naming_conventions: Public character lifecycle functions use the character prefix style, e.g. ftDrMario_OnDeath and ftDrMario_OnLoad.
Character attribute structs follow a character-specific name, here ftDrMarioAttributes, with offset-style member names when semantics are not yet known.
Unknown matched functions retained address-based names such as func_801497CC and func_80149954 because no stronger semantic names were evidenced.
Reviewer feedback says generic file suffixes like ftdrmario_2.c are discouraged when the Special can be identified; use a move-specific split such as SpecialN when evidence supports it.
The reviewer stated that ft files need to be split into respective Specials, establishing a preferred organization convention for fighter move files.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Deleted a 466-line assembly file only after replacing it with matched C objects in the build.
Created ftdrmario_2.c because func_80149954 needed a separate TU for correct relocations for a function pointer.
func_80149954 assigns func_80149540 into Fighter fields x21E4 and x21DC in the diff, matching the PR body's note about function-pointer relocation sensitivity.
Preserved stack-shaping artifacts such as int unused arrays and local temporaries where needed for matching.
Used explicit NULL-guard control flow in cleanup/query functions to mirror assembly branches.
Represented formerly sdata2 float constants directly as 3.0f and 0.0f in func_80149744 and func_80149788.
Converted item and projectile/effect calls around func_8026B3F8, func_802C0850, and func_802C0DBC into C while retaining unresolved callee names.

review_feedback.author: PsiLupan
review_feedback.feedback: Rather than name the new file '_2', determine which Special it starts, assumed possibly SpecialN, and name/split it accordingly because all ft files need to be split into their respective Specials.
review_feedback.status_from_slice: No evidence in this slice that the file was renamed before merge; ftdrmario_2.c remained in the changed files.

follow_up_queries: Was src/melee/ft/chara/ftdrmario_2.c later renamed to a Special-specific file such as SpecialN?
Which Dr. Mario Special does func_80149954 actually implement?
Did later PRs assign semantic names to func_80149540, func_801497CC, or the Fighter callback fields x21DC and x21E4?
Are there other fighter matches where function-pointer relocations required splitting a function into a separate translation unit?
Did later struct work clarify ftDrMarioAttributes fields x4, xC, and x14?
